title: Linear Programming and Constrained Optimization · Decision Notebook: Sensitivity Tradeoffs
summary: Choose a fair production and distribution plan under limited resources. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int sensitivity tradeoffs increment in an explainable constrained optimization model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Analyze active constraints, slack, parameter changes, and competing objectives.
Encode a constrained model for a solver and independently validate its solution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Sensitivity Tradeoffs evidence
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (4 × 7) − (1 × 2).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.
